hero.headline: 15+ years in web app and game development
hero.supportCopy: I build polished web applications that combine thoughtful front-end design with strong underlying systems.

about.heading: A background in games, teaching, and web development.
paragraphs: I started coding through game development in middle school and have now been building games and web applications for over 15 years. Along the way, I taught coding through charter school classes, a youth program, and adult workshops, while building many of my own projects from scratch. More recently, that work has included web projects for clients as well as ambitious application-focused projects of my own, including complex AI tools for teaching programming concepts.
That background shaped how I approach development now: with a strong focus on structure, usability, dynamic systems, and user experience.

focusAreas.label: Application Systems / UI
focusAreas.title: Web Applications
focusAreas.body: Full-stack web applications built to handle real users, content, and workflows.

focusAreas.label: Interactive / Dynamic Systems
focusAreas.title: Game Development
focusAreas.body: Game development taught me how interaction, movement, systems, and user experience need to work together.

focusAreas.label: Communication
focusAreas.title: Teaching / Education
focusAreas.body: Teaching coding shaped how I structure information, explain technical ideas, and design for clarity.

projects.heading: Featured Web Projects
projects.intro: Selected web projects from 2025 onward, spanning client work, content systems, and design-led application concepts.

items.title: Chez Max Dorothea

card.previewContext: Client-managed content with a modernized visual direction
card.meta: Live Site - React - Sanity CMS - Front-End Development
card.summary: A live React and Sanity website built to modernize an existing visual direction while keeping the final site manageable for client content updates.

overview: A live React and Sanity website built to modernize an existing visual direction while keeping the final site manageable for client content updates.
The project focused on responsive front-end implementation, structured CMS content, and preserving the overall feel of the site while bringing it into a cleaner, more modern format.
dialog.outcome: Shows live delivery work: front-end implementation, CMS structure, responsive behavior, and a finished site built for ongoing content updates.
contributions: React front-end implementation and component structure
Sanity CMS integration for client-managed content
Modernization of an existing visual direction

items.title: yourtechclass
items.meta: Education Site - Content Strategy - Design + Development

card.previewContext: Lesson offerings, teaching background, and a youth-focused visual identity
card.meta: Education Site - Content Strategy - Design + Development
card.summary: A personal site for my teaching services, built to present lesson offerings, past teaching experience, and a youth-focused visual identity that still works for all ages.

overview: A personal site for my teaching services, built to present lesson offerings, past teaching experience, and a youth-focused visual identity that still works for all ages.
The structure balances clarity, trust, and visual energy while still leaving room for adult learners, one-on-one instruction, and small group offerings.
dialog.outcome: Shows communication-heavy web design: structuring services clearly, presenting teaching experience, and building a site that feels approachable without losing clarity.

items.title: Su Xiaobai Foundation Redesign Concept
items.meta: Design Concept - Homepage System - Interactive Motion
items.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Su Xiaobai Foundation redesign concept

card.previewContext: Parallax, layered transitions, and homepage structure
card.meta: Design Concept - Homepage System - Interactive Motion
card.summary: A redesign concept created to modernize the foundation’s visual identity while keeping the feel of the original site. It uses parallax, layered transitions, and dynamic scrolling to show how design extends beyond static visuals into full user experience.

overview: A redesign concept created to modernize the foundation’s visual identity while keeping the feel of the original site.
It uses parallax, layered transitions, and dynamic scrolling to show how design extends beyond static visuals into full user experience.
dialog.outcome: Shows design-led front-end work where layout, motion, and section transitions all contribute to the user experience rather than acting as static visuals.
contributions: Homepage redesign concept and page structure direction
Motion and transition planning for scroll-based sections
Visual modernization while keeping the feel of the original site

interactive.heading: Games & Interactive Work
interactive.intro: A selection of projects that reflect my roots in game development and my continued interest in systems, motion, and interaction design.
interactive.legacyIntro: Before moving into professional web work, I spent years building games from scratch, handling gameplay logic, custom physics, and much of the art and animation myself.

ui.embeddedVideoNote: Embedded walkthrough video with instant stop on close.

featured.title: Rhythm Drumming VR Game
featured.meta: In Development - Web VR - Gameplay Systems
featured.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Rhythm Drumming VR Game project

card.summary: A rhythm-based drumming game in development for the web, combining timing-focused gameplay and performance-driven interaction in a more immersive format.
card.detail: The project explores timing accuracy, player feedback, movement rhythm, and a more physical interaction model than the rest of the portfolio currently shows.

dialog.summary: A rhythm-based drumming game in development for the web, combining timing-focused gameplay and performance-driven interaction in a more immersive format.
dialog.detail: The project explores timing accuracy, player feedback, movement rhythm, and a more physical interaction model than the rest of the portfolio currently shows.

legacy.title: Blood Red
legacy.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Blood Red game project

card.summary: This project was useful for combat feedback, enemy pressure, and the feel of fast-response weapons.

dialog.summary: A zombie survival shooter built from scratch, where I explored hit reactions, dynamic animation combinations, and instant bullet collision detection along a traced line.
dialog.detail: This project was useful for combat feedback, enemy pressure, and the feel of fast-response weapons.

legacy.title: Toxic Tides
legacy.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Toxic Tides game project

card.summary: It pushed on pacing under load, handling multiple threats, and keeping the screen readable during heavier action.

dialog.summary: An underwater multidirectional shooter focused on wave survival, enemy variety, and performance optimization for large numbers of active enemies on screen.
dialog.detail: It pushed on pacing under load, handling multiple threats, and keeping the screen readable during heavier action.

legacy.title: Droid Team
legacy.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Droid Team game project

card.summary: This one shows a different side of the work: puzzle communication, rule clarity, and constraint-driven design.

dialog.summary: A puzzle game built around custom character physics, stacking mechanics, and level design that forced cooperative problem-solving without allowing players to bypass the intended challenge.
dialog.detail: This one shows a different side of the work: puzzle communication, rule clarity, and constraint-driven design.

skills.heading: Capabilities
skills.intro: Selected projects shown here represent a focused sample of my work. Additional experience details and samples are available upon request.

groups.title: Full Stack Web Development
groups.label: Product-minded build work
groups.summary: I build complete web experiences across interface development, application structure, responsive systems, and the integration work needed to ship something stable and usable.

groups.title: Educator / Coding Curriculum
groups.label: Clear technical teaching
groups.summary: My teaching background includes coding instruction, curriculum development, and breaking complex ideas into approachable lessons without flattening the real substance.

groups.title: App And Game Development
groups.label: Systems and interaction
groups.summary: Apps and games have been the strongest training ground for systems thinking, interaction design, mechanics, and the kind of iteration required to make behavior feel right.

contact.heading: If the work feels aligned, let's talk.
contact.body: I'm interested in roles and collaborations where front-end craft, interaction quality, and clear implementation all need to work together.
contact.availability: Open to front-end, interactive, and design-sensitive development work.
